meta.title: A math wordle alternative for math people with one puzzle a day
meta.description: You have to find a simple addition or subtraction in less than 5 tries. Each guess must be a valid equation. Use a smart strategy and share the result.
seo.heading: Mathle, a daily math Wordle game
seo.intro: Mathle is a daily number puzzle for people who enjoy Wordle-style logic and quick arithmetic. Each puzzle asks you to find one hidden addition or subtraction equation.
seo.howToPlay: Mathle gives you five tries to uncover one hidden addition or subtraction. Every guess must be a complete equation, so you are solving both the arithmetic and the exact placement of each character.
seo.howToPlayRule1: Use digits with one plus or minus sign and the fixed equals sign. The answer side is always a positive number.
seo.howToPlayRule2: Green means the character is correct and in the right position. Yellow means it appears in the equation but belongs somewhere else. Gray means it is not in the solution.
seo.howToPlayRule3: A strong opening guess tests different digits and one operator. After each row, combine the color feedback with arithmetic rules to narrow the remaining equations.
seo.howToPlayRule4: You win when your equation exactly matches the hidden Mathle. If the fifth guess is wrong, the solution is revealed.
seo.howToPlayRule5Title: Use Color Blind Mode if needed
seo.howToPlayRule5: Color Blind Mode changes the feedback palette: correct-position green becomes amber, present-elsewhere yellow becomes blue, and absent gray stays gray.
seo.exampleGreen: The plus sign is green because it is in the correct position.
seo.exampleYellow: The digit 6 is yellow because it appears in the equation, but it belongs somewhere else.
seo.exampleGray: The digit 5 is gray because it is not part of the solution.
seo.colorBlindPalette: Color Blind Mode keeps the same meaning but changes the palette: correct becomes amber, present becomes blue, and absent stays gray.
seo.daily: You have five guesses to solve the daily Mathle. Your progress is saved in this browser, and a new puzzle becomes available after midnight.
seo.modes: Daily mode gives everyone one shared puzzle each day, Hourly mode adds a fresh challenge every hour, and Infinite mode lets you keep practicing whenever you want another arithmetic puzzle.
seo.strategy: Start with a valid equation that uses a mix of digits and one operator. Use the color feedback to narrow down the exact position of each number, plus sign, or minus sign before your five guesses run out.
seo.practice: Mathle is designed for short sessions: solve one puzzle on a break, compare your result with friends, or use Infinite mode to warm up your mental math skills.
seo.faqAnswer1: Mathle is a Wordle-style math puzzle where you guess a hidden addition or subtraction equation in five tries.
seo.faqQuestion2: Is there a new Mathle every day?
seo.faqAnswer2: Yes. Daily mode gives one browser-local puzzle each day, while Hourly mode refreshes every hour and Infinite mode lets you keep practicing.
seo.faqQuestion3: Does each guess need to be a real equation?
seo.faqAnswer3: Yes. Every guess must be a valid equation using digits, one plus or minus sign, and the fixed equals sign.
seo.faqAnswer4: Green marks a character in the correct position, yellow marks a character that belongs somewhere else, and gray marks a character that is not in the answer.
seo.faqQuestion5: What is a good Mathle strategy?
seo.faqAnswer5: Start with a valid equation that uses a mix of digits and one operator. Then combine the color feedback with arithmetic rules to narrow the possible equations.
seo.faqQuestion6: Can I practice without waiting for tomorrow?
seo.faqAnswer6: Yes. Infinite mode lets you start new browser-generated puzzles whenever you want, and Hourly mode gives a fresh scheduled puzzle every hour.
seo.faqQuestion7: What does Color Blind Mode change?
seo.faqAnswer7: Color Blind Mode replaces the standard green and yellow feedback with amber and blue, while gray still marks characters that are not in the answer.
seo.noscript: Mathle needs JavaScript to run the interactive puzzle. The rules are available here, but the game board requires JavaScript.
